messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
What is the nationality of the Tour 2017 celebrity participant with the least number of shows in Danse avec les stars – La tournée

content.message:
I need to find the nationality of the Tour 2017 celebrity participant with the least number of shows in "Danse avec les stars – La tournée".

Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to identify what "Danse avec les stars – La tournée" is in Wikidata. This is likely a specific tour or season of the French show.

2. I need to identify the celebrity participants from the 2017 tour.

3. I need to determine how many shows each participant had.

4. Find the participant with the least number of shows.

5. Find the nationality of that participant.

To start, let me search for information about "Danse avec les stars – La tournée" in Wikidata.

tool_calls.name: search_entity

args.query: Danse avec les stars – La tournée

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Le soir – La danse des nymphes (wd:Q90924047):
  - is instance of painting
  - painting by Jean-Baptiste Camille Corot
2. Category:Danse avec les stars contestants (wd:Q26773744):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
3. Category:Danse avec les stars (wd:Q15319897):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
4. Danse avec les stars (wd:Q1660463), also known as "French Dancing with the Stars":
  - French television program
  - is instance of television program
  - is subclass of competition
5. Template:Danse avec les stars (wd:Q20374253), also known as "Template:Danse Avec Les Stars 2011", "Template:Danse Avec Les Stars Season 2", "Template:Danse Avec Les Stars Season 3", "Template:Danse Avec Les Stars Season 4", "Template:Danse Avec Les Stars Season 5", etc.:
  - is instance of Wikimedia template
  - Wikipedia template page
6. Danse avec les stars, season 5 (wd:Q17177000):
  - is instance of television series season
  - season of television series

content.message:
I need to refine my search. From the results, I can see that "Danse avec les stars" is a French television program, and there are specific seasons. The most relevant result is "Danse avec les stars (wd:Q1660463)" which is the French television program "Dancing with the Stars". 

The question specifically mentions "La tournée" (the tour) in 2017. Since the entity search didn't return "La tournée" specifically, I should look for information about the 2017 season or tour of "Danse avec les stars".

Let me search for information about the 2017 season of Danse avec les stars.

I need to try a different approach. Let me search for the specific season of Danse avec les stars that might correspond to 2017, or look for properties related to the show that might contain tour information.

Let me search for properties related to "Danse avec les stars" that might contain information about tours or seasons.

tool_calls.result:

1. subclass of (wd:P279 as wdt/p, matched via "way of"), also known as "all members of this class also belong to", "category of", "form of", "has superclass", "is a class of", etc.:

2. start time (wd:P580 as wdt/p, matched via "from"), also known as "air date", "began", "began airing", "began broadcasting", "began streaming", etc.:

3. country of origin (wd:P495 as wdt/p, matched via "country"), also known as "comes from", "CoO", "country", "homeland", "originates from", etc.:
  - country of origin of this item (creative work, food, phrase, product, etc.)
4. original broadcaster (wd:P449 as wdt/p, matched via "airs on"), also known as "aired on", "airs on", "broadcast on", "channel", "first air channel", etc.:
  - network(s) or service(s) that originally broadcast a radio or television program
5. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
6. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "type"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:

7. official website (wd:P856 as wdt/p, matched via "homepage"), also known as "home page", "homepage", "home page (official)", "homepage (official)", "official homepage", etc.:

8. logo image (wd:P154 as wdt/p, matched via "marque"), also known as "insignia", "logo", "logomark", "mark", "marque", etc.:

9. presenter (wd:P371 as wdt/p, matched via "event presenter"), also known as "event presenter", "host", "hosted by", "hostess", "presented by", etc.:
  - main role in presenting a radio or television program or a performing arts show
10. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p, matched via "champ"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. participant (wd:P710 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "accused", "agent", "attendee", "belligerents", "between", etc.:

2. number of participants (wd:P1132 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "participants"), also known as "number of competitors", "number of teams", "participant count", "participants", "qty participants":
  - number of participants of an event, e.g. people or groups of people that take part in the event (NO units)
3. participant in (wd:P1344 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "attended", "competed in", "competes in", "participant of", "participant of event", etc.:
  - event in which a person, organization or creative work was/is a participant; inverse of P710 or P1923
4. spouse (wd:P26 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "partner"), also known as "common-law husband", "common-law spouse", "common-law wife", "husband", "husbands", etc.:
  - the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, ... Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions

  - someone with whom the person is in a relationship without being married. Use "spouse" (P26) for married couples
6. recorded participant (wd:P11108 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "participant recorded"), also known as "on-screen participant", "onscreen participant", "participant recorded":
  - agent taking an active role as a participant in a nonfiction moving image or other recorded work
7. member of political party (wd:P102 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "party"), also known as "member of", "member of party", "party", "party membership", "political party", etc.:
  - the political party of which a person is or has been a member or otherwise affiliated
8. parliamentary group (wd:P4100 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "party"), also known as "faction", "party", "party organization":
  - parliamentary group to which a member of a parliament belongs
9. proportion (wd:P1107 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "part"), also known as "%", "part", "percent", "ratio", "share":
  - to be used as a qualifier, value must be between 0 and 1
10. candidate (wd:P726 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "candidate in this election", "election has candidate", "electoral candidate", "has election candidate":
